description: Render a CSV (first column = x, remaining columns = y series) into a publication-styled matplotlib line figure and save it in the requested formats/DPI via save_publication_figure().
rules: read-only with respect to inputs; only writes under record_store
first CSV column is x, every remaining numeric column is a plotted y series
FORMATS defaults to pdf,png and DPI defaults to 300; vector formats cap embedded-raster DPI at 300
degrades gracefully (writes a non-empty manifest, exits 0) when matplotlib/numpy are absent
usage: Set DATA_CSV (CSV with header + numeric columns). Optional FORMATS (comma list) and DPI. Outputs: figure_export.json manifest plus figure.<fmt> files.
limitation: Produces a simple multi-series line plot only; for bespoke multi-panel layouts call matplotlib directly. Figure files require matplotlib + numpy present.
